content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 21 , ]-99L
Burton w. oliver
Executive Director
State Board of Equalization
1020 N Street
Sacramento, CA 9 5814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-91-259a
Dear Mr. oliver:
You have requested advice on behalf of the members of the
State Board of Equalization (rtthe Boardr') regarding the
application of the rnass mailing provisions of the Political- Reform
att (tt" "Acttr).1 This Ietter provides clarification 
of advice
previously provided to EarI J. cantos, Jr., as a representative of
tarnest J. bronenburg, Jr., a member of the Board- (cantos Advice
Letter No. I-91-2 59 . )
OUESTIONS
1. If no more than 200 copies of the annual- report are
rnailed in a single calendar month, does Regulation 18901 apply?
2. May each member independently distribute 200 copies or
less of the same item each nonth without violating Regulation
18 9 01?
CONCLUSIONS
1. If no more than 200 copies of the annual report are
rnailed in a single calendar month, Regulation 18901 does not
appty. Therefore, the annual report could include a picture of
each rnember, a biography, and a l-etter signed by the current
chairman of the Board.
1' 
covernment code Sections 8l-000-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment code unl,ess otherwi.se indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regul-ations section
18000, et ses. A11 references to regulations are to Titfe 2
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations,
428 J Street, Suite 800 . P.O. Box 807 o Sacramento, CA 958044807 O (gfq 322.5660


Our File No. A-9■
-259a
Page 2
2. Each menber nay independently distribute 2o0 copies or
less of the same item each month without violating Regulation
18901.
FACTS
Prj.or to the passage of Proposition 73, the annual report of
the state Board of Equalization included pictures of each member,
a biography, and a letter signed by the current chairman. with
the passage of Proposition 73, these items were deleted from the
annuaL report pending clarification 
of the new prohibitj-ons on
mass mailings as public expense. The Board is now preparing the
annual report for l-990-90 which will include pictures, a signed
letter from the chairman, and biographies of the individual
members of the Board.
Most copies of the annual report wiII be distributed within
the agency, to officers of other agencies, or pursuant to
unsolicited requests from menbers of the public. 
Approxinately
370 copies will be provided to the individual members for
independent distribution.
ANALYSTS
The Act provides that a newsletter or other mass mailing2 nay
not be sent at public expense. (section 89001') The Comrnission
has interpreted this provision of the Act to apply only to mass
nailings which include the names, photographs, signatures or other
references to elected officers. 
(Regulation 18901')
Regulation 18901 provides that a mailing is prohibited only
if alL of the following applY:
(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to
the recipient at his or her resi,dence, place of
employment or business, or post office box. For
purposes of this subdivision (a) (1), the iten delivered
to the recipient rnust be a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2, The iten sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated
rrith the agency rrhich produces or sends the
mailing, or
2 A ..=r nairing
s irni lar pieces of
other nail which
other inquiry.tr
is defined as l:over
mail′ but does not
is sent in response
(SeCtiOn 8204■
.5。
)
ts/o hundred substantialLy
include a form letter or
to an unsolicited request


Our File No. A-9■-259a
Page 3
(B) Includes the narne, office, photograph, or
other reference to an elected officer affiliated
with the agency which produces or sends the
rnailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.
(3) (A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid
for with public moneys; or
(B) costs of design, production, and printing
exceeding $5o.oo are paid with public moneys, and
the design, production, or prj,nting is done with
the intent of sending the itern other than as
permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than tl^ro hundred substantially sinilar
items are sent, in a single calendar rnonth, excluding
any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b).
Regulation 18901(a) .
Thus, if 2oo or less copies of an item are sent in a calendar
month, the mass maiting prohibitions do not apply. As we
previously advised, such a rnailing may include the name,
photograph, signature or other reference to an elected officer
without violating the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act.
(cantos Advice Letter, supra, page 4.)
Therefore, if only 200 or less copj.es of the Board's Annual
Report are distributed in a calendar month, the report may contain
the members photographs and biographj.es and may include a Letter
sj.gned by the current chairman.
In tallying the number of copies distributed, copies hrhich
are otherwise exempted under subdivision (b) of Regulation 18901
are lq! counted. This would include, for example, annual reports
sent to Board employees, officers, deputies and other staff in the
normal course of business (18901(b) (4)); annual reports sent in
the normal course of business fron the Board to another
governnental entity or officer (18901(b) (3)); and annual reports
sent to members of the public pursuant to unsolicited requests
(18e01(a) (4) ) .
Final1y, where elected state officers, such as rflembers of the
Board (Sections 82021 and 82024) send items independently, iterns
sent by one nember will not be curnulated with iteurs sent by
another menber. Thus, each member may independently distribute


Our Fi■e No. A-9■-259a
Page 4
200 or less copies of the annual report each month without
violating Regulation 18901. r
I trust that this clarifies our advice with respect to
distribution of your annual report consistent rrith the nass
nailing provisions of the Act. If you have any further questions
or require any further clarification, 
you may contact ne at (916)
322-59 Ot .1
S incere Iy,
scott HaIlabrin,
Acting General counsel
By: llargaret w. Ellison
counsel, Legal Division
SH:MWE
a" we would again advise you, ho$rever, that if a coordinated
mailing $rere sent by members of the Board in an effort to
circunvent the mass rnailing provisions, the total number of itens
sent would be cumulated for purposes of deternining whether the
nailing is a rrnass mailing.tr
- copies of Corunission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries. 
Alternatively, copies of these naterials and
Comrnission advice letters nay be obtained from the Cornrnission at a
cost of 10C per page.
